content:
# Geophysics, Applied

**Summary**: The application of geophysical methods to solve practical problems in the earth sciences.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2009-al-lehyani|Al-Lehyani, 2009]] addressed a problem in applied-geophysics regarding land seismic exploration.

- [[2013-mark|Mark, 2013]] used seismic attributes and SVM to predict pore pressure.

- [[2014-saeed|Saeed, 2014]] used applied-geophysics techniques to characterize subsurface cavities.

- [[2016-edigbue|Edigbue, 2016]] addressed applied-geophysics challenges by developing a 3D supervirtual algorithm for seismic data enhancement.

- [[2018-putra|Putra, 2018]] Referenced in this thesis.

- [[2020-alzahrani|Alzahrani, 2020]] utilized applied-geophysics techniques for seismic data processing and analysis.

- [[2021-almashhor|AlMashhor, 2021]] developed automated tools for seismic data processing.

- [[2021-shaheen|Shaheen, 2021]] employed applied-geophysics techniques, including seismic monitoring and deep learning, for hazard assessment.

- [[2022-awais|Awais, 2022]] applied geophysics techniques like super-virtual refraction interferometry for seismic data processing.

- [[2023-edigbue|Edigbue, 2023]] used various applied geophysics techniques for subsurface exploration and modeling.

- [[2023-bargees|Bargees, 2023]] applied geophysics techniques to develop a robust automated picking algorithm for industrial data.

- [[2024-jahlan|Jahlan, 2024]] addressed the problem of sand dune noise using advanced signal processing techniques.

- [[2009-al-lehyani|Al-Lehyani, 2009]] ['seismic processing', 'signal analysis']

## Methods and Approaches Used

- [[2009-al-lehyani|Al-Lehyani, 2009]] ['mathematical modeling']

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Not stated in source ([[2009-al-lehyani|Al-Lehyani, 2009]])

- SVM is faster than traditional seismic velocity modeling for prediction. ([[2013-mark|Mark, 2013, p. 104]])

- The joint inversion approach is validated to estimate porosity and water saturation for the material inside a cavity. ([[2014-saeed|Saeed, 2014, p. 55]])

- The method successfully improved SNR from 4 to 20, demonstrating its utility for real-world noisy seismic records. ([[2016-edigbue|Edigbue, 2016, p. 60]])

- Referenced in this thesis. ([[2018-putra|Putra, 2018]])

- The squaring technique provides another method for picking stacking velocities and is comparable and compatible with conventional methods. ([[2020-alzahrani|Alzahrani, 2020, p. 17]])

- Automated picking reduced human error and improved efficiency in static corrections. ([[2021-almashhor|AlMashhor, 2021, p. 1]])

- Automated detection of low-magnitude events is crucial for industrial applications like CCS and geothermal monitoring. ([[2021-shaheen|Shaheen, 2021, p. 24]])

- Not stated in source ([[2022-awais|Awais, 2022]])

- The joint inversion of DCR and SR data has become a common technique for subsurface exploration due to its high-resolution imaging capabilities. ([[2023-edigbue|Edigbue, 2023, p. 33]])

- Automated picking is essential for large volumes of seismic data where manual picking is not feasible. ([[2023-bargees|Bargees, 2023, p. 19]])

- The study demonstrated that parallax-based methods provide a more robust way to handle localized occlusions than standard filtering. ([[2024-jahlan|Jahlan, 2024, p. 42]])

## Competing Approaches and Debates

- [[2009-al-lehyani|Al-Lehyani, 2009]] Identifying and removing noise from land seismic data.
